title: HLA-A 5'UTR/promoter is dramatically cell-type-specific — monocyte β=0.083 (open) vs T-naive β=0.593 (closed), a Δβ=0.510 inversion that explains the Phase 4c paradox

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'HLA-A 5'UTR/promoter is dramatically cell-type-specific — monocyte β=0.083 (open'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Loyfer et al. 2023 WGBS methylation atlas at 1 kb resolution, 8+ hematopoietic cell types including monocyte, B naive, B memory, T naive, T memory, NK, erythroid, HSC, CMP, GMP, neutrophil, eosinophil.
HLA-A reference-genome 1 kb window coordinates from chr6:29,941,000–29,942,000 spanning the 5'UTR and proximal promoter. Contains 25 CpGs.

operations.note: Per-cell-type mean β across the 25 CpGs in the 5'UTR/promoter window.
Pull the monocyte and T-naive means for the cross-cell-type comparison.

statistics.name: hla_a_5utr_promoter_mean_beta_monocyte
statistics.note: Monocyte HLA-A 5'UTR/promoter β = 0.083 — open (unmethylated). HLA-A is transcribed in monocytes, consistent with antigen-presentation function.

statistics.name: hla_a_5utr_promoter_mean_beta_t_naive
statistics.note: T-naive HLA-A 5'UTR/promoter β = 0.593 — intermediate/closed. Naive T cells transcribe HLA-A less than effector populations; the promoter is partially methylated.

statistics.name: t_naive_minus_monocyte_beta
statistics.note: Derived: s_t_naive_beta − s_monocyte_beta = 0.593 − 0.083 = 0.510. An exceptionally large cross-cell-type Δβ at a single 1 kb promoter window — far exceeds typical tissue-of-the-same-lineage comparisons.

statistics.name: hla_a_5utr_promoter_mean_beta_average
statistics.note: Average across all 12 cell types. The mean is near 0.3 because the two extremes (monocyte 0.08, T-naive 0.59) balance; the distribution itself is bimodal.

statistics.name: hla_a_exon_1_2_cgi_mean_beta_average
statistics.note: The adjacent exon 1–2 CpG island (10 CpGs, 29,942–29,943k) is uniformly unmethylated (mean β = 0.031) across ALL cell types. This is the internal positive control: promoter-proximal CpG islands at active genes should be constitutively unmethylated. Its flatness highlights that the 5'UTR/promoter variability is genuine and not a measurement artifact.

inference.justification: Four conjuncts license the bimodal cell-type-specific methylation claim: (1) monocyte β < 0.2 — the 'open' end is substantively unmethylated; (2) T-naive β > 0.5 — the 'closed' end is substantively methylated; (3) Δβ > 0.3 — the inter-celltype swing is large, consistent with an actively regulated locus (typical between-tissue Δβ at housekeeping promoters is < 0.1); (4) exon 1-2 CGI β < monocyte β — stat-vs-stat internal control: the adjacent constitutive CpG island is even less methylated than monocytes, so the 5'UTR/promoter variability is a local feature of THIS window, not a global measurement artifact.
inference.failure_mode: If Δβ < 0.3, the 'dramatic cell-type specificity' framing weakens to 'typical tissue variation'. If the exon 1-2 CGI were heterogeneously methylated, the result at the 5'UTR could be confounded with technical variance. The CGI's flatness is the silent pinned anchor.

conclusion.assertion: HLA-A's 5'UTR/promoter (1 kb window containing 25 CpGs at chr6:29,941,000–29,942,000) is dramatically cell-type specific in its methylation state: monocyte mean β = 0.083 (open) vs T-naive mean β = 0.593 (closed), a Δβ of 0.510. The adjacent exon 1-2 CpG island is uniformly unmethylated (β = 0.031) across all cell types, serving as an internal control that validates the promoter-region variability. This cell-type-specific pattern is where the Ramsuran 2015 allele-specific methylation operates and explains the Phase 4c paradox in exp24 — HLA-A expression differences by allele are modulated by a background of strong cell-type methylation asymmetry.

scope.scale_note: Reference-genome WGBS, NOT allele-resolved. The Ramsuran 2015 allele-specific component (A*24:02 vs A*01:01 methylation in monocytes) is a separate child claim because it requires allele-assigned reads that Loyfer does not provide.

confidence.note: Per-celltype n=25 CpGs; no formal inferential test — this is a descriptive per-window comparison. Per-CpG permutation null staged but not pinned.

external_assumptions.statement: Loyfer 2023 WGBS β values at 1 kb resolution are faithful representations of methylation state in the named cell types; reference-genome alignment is appropriate for a species-conserved locus like HLA-A.

Reference-only methylation is a limitation — per-allele methylation differences are superimposed on the cross-celltype pattern reported here. The cross-celltype Δβ should be interpreted as the population-average modulation within which allele-specific patterns operate.
The 1 kb window at chr6:29,941,000–29,942,000 faithfully captures the HLA-A 5'UTR and proximal promoter region; annotation is consistent with RefSeq and GENCODE.
